title: Lush Herbal Japanese Mackerel Sushi
description: A fragrant herb‑fused mackerel ehomaki with juicy sashimi, bright greens and a silky soy‑sesame glaze, perfect for a brunch‑mood lunch.

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: whisked with soy sauce and chili

instructions: Rinse the sushi rice until water runs clear, then cook it with water according to package instructions.
Whisk together rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until the sugar dissolves.
Transfer the cooked rice to a bowl and fold in the vinegar mixture, letting it cool to room temperature.
Combine the chopped dill and cilantro with the rice, mixing gently.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat, add a splash of oil, and sear the mackerel fillets for 2 minutes on each side until just cooked through.
Remove the fish from the skillet, let it rest for a minute, then thinly slice it lengthwise into strips.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, and chili flakes together in a small bowl to create a quick glaze.
Lay a sheet of nori on a bamboo sushi mat, wet your hands with water, spread a thin layer of the herb‑rice mixture across the nori leaving a 1 cm border at the top.
Arrange the sliced mackerel and a drizzle of the soy‑sesame glaze over the rice, then roll the mat tightly to form a cylinder.
Slice the ehomaki into 8 equal rounds and sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the top before serving.
